column3:
覚者さま、嬉しいけど――
これじゃ、<COL b92828><NAME ITEM></COL>足りないよ
<COL b92828><VAL> 個</COL>貰えるまで
我慢して待ってますね
column4:
I'm happy, Arisen, but...
This isn't enough <COL b92828><NAME ITEM></COL> yet.
I'll be patient and wait until I can have <COL b92828><VAL></COL> of them, okay?

column3:
実は、覚者様に見せたいものがあるんです！
しばらく外を散歩してきてください！
column4:
Actually, there's something I'd like to show you, Arisen!
Please, go take a walk outside for a little while!

column3:
覚者さま――
本当にぼくがお傍にいなくて――いいんですか？
column4:
Arisen...
Are you truly certain... that you're alright without me by your side?

column3:
覚者さま、今日の調子はいかがですか？
ぼくはとっても元気ですよ！
column4:
Arisen, how are you feeling today?
I'm doing absolutely wonderful!

column3:
覚者さま、とっても良い天気です！
ね、外に行きましょうよ！
column4:
Arisen, the weather is absolutely wonderful!
Come on, let's head outside!

column3:
連れているポーンよりも覚者さまがすごく強いと
ポーンの経験量がたくさん増える――みたいです
ってことは、すぐ強くなれちゃいますね！　すごい！
column4:
It seems that if the Arisen is much stronger than
the Pawns with them, those Pawns will earn a lot
more experience... which means I'll be able to get
strong in no time! That's amazing!

column3:
覚者さま、お出かけですか？
わぁい、今日は何が見られるんだろう
たのしみだなぁ！
column4:
Are you heading out, Arisen? Yay! I wonder what
we'll get to see today... I'm so excited!

column3:
お帰りなさい、覚者さま！
ごはんにしますか？　おふろにしますか？
それとも――座ってのんびりしちゃいます？
column4:
Welcome back, Arisen!
Would you like to have dinner? A bath? Or... would
you rather just sit down and relax for a bit?

column3:
はい！
何かご用ですか、覚者さま？
column4:
Yes!
Did you need something, Arisen?

column3:
はぁ――外に出るのも楽しいけど
ずっとお部屋でのんびりしてるのもいいなぁ
安全なお部屋にずっといたいなんて――
ぼく、本当にポーンなのかな？
column4:
Sigh... Going outside is fun and all, but...
Just lounging around the room is nice, too.
Wanting to stay in a safe room forever—
I wonder... am I really a Pawn?

column3:
覚者さま、お耳を貸してください！
あのね――ぼく、覚者さまのポーンでよかったです！
column4:
Arisen, could you lend me your ear for a moment?
You know... I'm really glad that I'm your Pawn!

column3:
ポーンは戦うための存在だって
そう思ってたけど――
ぼくをこんなすてきなお部屋に入れてくださって
ありがとうございます、覚者さま！
column4:
I always thought that
Pawns existed only to fight...
But you've given me such a wonderful room
to stay in. Thank you so much, Arisen!

column3:
ふぁぁ――
ん、何ですかぁ？
column4:
Yawn...
Hmm? Did you... need something...?

column3:
えへへ――
呼ばれてみただけ！
column4:
Ehehe...
I just wanted to see if you'd answer, that's all!

column3:
ねぇねぇ、覚者さま
ぼく――ううん、なんでもないです！
覚者さまの笑顔を見たら、元気になっちゃいました！
column4:
Hey, Arisen, I... No, never mind! Just seeing
your smile has given me all the energy I need!

column3:
あっ！　ちょうど今、次は覚者さまと
どこに行きたいかな、って考えてたんです
column4:
Oh! I was just now thinking about
where I'd like to go with you next, Arisen!

column3: あははは！　くすぐったいです覚者さま！
column4: Ahahaha! That tickles, Arisen!

column3:
覚者さま、今日も冒険ですか？
ううん、冒険もいいんですけど――
たまにはお部屋でゆっくりしたいなぁ
column4:
Off on another adventure today, Arisen?
Well... adventuring is all well and good, but—
I'd really love to just relax here in the room
for a change.

column3:
あっ、覚者さま！　どこに行くんですか？
ぼくも連れて行ってくれますよね？
お留守番は嫌ですよ！
column4:
Ah, Arisen! Where are you going?
You're taking me with you, right?
Please don't make me stay behind—
I hate being left home alone!

column3:
今日もお出かけですか？
この前みたいに怪我、しないでくださいね
ぼくと約束ですよ
column4:
Heading out again today?
Please... don't get hurt
like you did the other day.
Promise me, okay?

column3:
はい、覚者様！
ぼくはここにいます
column4:
Yes, Arisen!
I'm right here by your side.

column3:
ふふふ、覚者さま！　くすぐったいです！
でも――覚者さまの手って、温かいですね
column4:
Hehehe! That tickles, Arisen!
But... your hands are so warm...

column3:
覚者さま、ぼくはずっとここにいますよ
慌てなくても、大丈夫ですからね
column4:
I'm right here, Arisen. I'm not going anywhere.
There's no need to rush—
everything is going to be just fine.

column3:
なんと昨日、お庭にグリフィンが来てたんです
仲良くなれたから、いーっぱい遊んじゃいました！
あやしてあげると安心するみたいですよ
覚者さまも試してみてください！
column4:
Guess what, Arisen?
A Griffin came to the garden yesterday!
We actually became friends,
so we played together for a long, long time!
It seems like they feel much calmer
if you soothe them.
You should try it yourself sometime, Arisen!

column3:
動物と仲良くなる方法ですか？ うーん――
お庭に向かって、何かしてみたらどうですか？
たとえば、エモートとか？
反応してくれるかはわからないけど――
column4:
How to get along with animals? Hmm...
Why don't you try doing something
out in the garden?
Like... using an emote, maybe?
I'm not sure if they'll react to it,
but it's worth a try!

column3:
一緒に冒険に行ったり
クラフトを任されたりすると
覚者さまのこと、もっともっと
わかるような気がします！
column4:
When we go on adventures together,
or when you entrust me with crafting...
I feel like I'm getting to know you better and
better, Arisen!

column3:
レスタニアンパペットがあれば
お留守番で寂しいとき、
一緒に遊んで待てるんだけどなぁ――
column4:
If only I had a Lestanian Puppet...
Then I could play with it and wouldn't feel so
lonely while I'm waiting at home for you.

column3:
クラフトする時は
任せるポーンを間違えないでくださいね！
ぼくらポーンにも、得意なことと
苦手なことがありますから
column4:
When it comes to crafting, please make sure
you choose the right Pawn for the job!
We Pawns have our own strengths and weaknesses,
you know.

column3:
神殿の外でも
戦技の切り替えができるそうですよ！
便利ですね！
column4:
I've heard we can swap our combat skills
even outside the Temple now!
That's certainly convenient, isn't it?

column3:
覚者さまみたいに
気持ちを体で表現できるようになりたくて
いつもこっそり見てるんです！
column4:
I want to be able to express my feelings
through my body, just like you do, Arisen!
That's why I'm always...
well, secretly watching you to learn how!

column3:
ねぇ、覚者さま――気付いてないかもしれないけど
ぼくがたくさん色んなものを見られるのは
覚者さまのおかげだって、すっごく感謝してるんです
column4:
Hey, Arisen... You might not realize it,
but I'm so incredibly grateful to you.
It's only because of you that I've been able to
see so much of the world.

column3:
覚者さまも、隣にいるポーンが
かっこ悪かったら嫌ですよね？
だからぼく、見た目には結構気を付けてるんです
column4:
You wouldn't want the Pawn by your side to look
uncool, would you, Arisen? That's why I actually
put quite a bit of effort into my appearance!

column3:
ねぇねぇ覚者さま！
《鍛錬の小部屋》、使ってますか？
えへへ――ぼくが覚者さまのために
毎日お掃除してるんですよー！
column4:
Hey, hey, Arisen! Have you been using the
Chamber of Training? Hehe—I've actually been
cleaning it every single day, just for you!

column3: さあ、行きましょう！
column4: Come, let us be off!

column3: お願い聞いてくれないんですか？
column4: Won't you listen to my request?

column3: 早くいきましょう！
column4: Hurry, Arisen! Let's get going!

column3:
わーい！　ありがとうございます！
いつくださるんですか？！
もしかして、今ですか？　早く早くー
column4:
Yay! Thank you so much!
When are you giving it to me?!
Wait—is it right now?
Hurry, hurry! I can't wait!

column3:
えー！
そうですか――しかたないですけど――
うぅ――覚者さまのケチ
column4:
What?! Are you serious? I mean... if you say so,
I guess I have no choice... whimper...
You're so stingy, Arisen!

column3:
わーい！　ぼくにくれるんですよね？
ありがとうございます！
column4:
Whoo-hoo! You're really giving it to me?
Thank you! Thank you so much, Arisen!

column3:
ねぇ覚者さま、お願いしたアレはまだですか？
ぼく、ずっと楽しみにしてるんです！
column4:
Hey, Arisen... 
Is that 'thing' I asked for ready yet?
I've been looking forward to it this whole time!

column3:
覚者さま、おかえりなさい！
ウェアをお洗濯しておきました
たぶん大丈夫だと思うんですけど――
濡れてたらごめんなさい！
column4:
Welcome home, Arisen!
I've finished washing your gear for you.
I think it's all dry, but...
I'm sorry if it's still a little damp!

column3:
覚者さま、おかえりなさい！
お掃除とか、お洗濯とか、がんばったんです
ねぇ、えらいですか？
へへ――だってぼく、パートナーポーンですから！
column4:
Welcome home, Arisen! I've been working so hard
on the cleaning and the laundry.
Tell me... did I do a good job?
Hehe—after all, I am your Partner Pawn!

column3:
覚者さまって――いつか竜になっちゃうんですか？
そうしたら――ぼくたちポーンは
どこへ行っちゃうんでしょう？
ぼくのこと、忘れちゃったりしないですよね――？
column4:
Arisen...
is it true that you'll become a dragon one day?
If that happens... where will we Pawns go?
You won't... you won't forget about me, will you?

column3:
ねぇねぇ、ぼくってもう人みたいだと思いませんか？
へへ――いつも覚者さまを観察していましたからね
いろんな表現だってできるんですよ！
column4:
Hey, hey, Arisen! Don't you think I'm starting to
seem more... human? Hehe—I've been watching you
so closely all this time, you see.
Now I can express myself in all sorts of ways!

column3:
覚者さまが一番好きな食べもの――
ぼくもいっしょに楽しんでみたいなぁ
最近、覚者さまや人が楽しむものに
興味があるんです！
column4:
Your favorite food, Arisen...
I'd love to try it with you!
Lately, I've found myself so curious about
the things that people—and you—enjoy!

column3:
ぼく、もっと覚者さまと色んなところへ行ったり
色んなものを見たりしたいんです！
――ん？　何だか今、人間みたいなこと
言っちゃいましたね
column4:
Arisen, I... I want to go so many more places
with you, and see so many more things!
Oh—did you hear that? I sounded almost...
human just now, didn't I?

column3:
ねぇねぇ覚者さま、ぼく本を読んだんです！
えっと、確か――植物さんは、お肉や皮、野菜とか
人間も大好きなものを食べておおきくなるんだって
えっと、あとは――ごめんなさい、覚えてないや！
column4:
Hey, hey, Arisen! I read a book!
Let's see... I think it said that plants grow big
by eating meat, hides, and vegetables...
all the things people love to eat, too!
And then... um... I'm sorry,
I don't remember the rest!

column3:
覚者さま、いつでもぼくをポーン遠征隊で
派遣していいんですからね！
クランに入ってるなら、ちゃんと毎日派遣したほうが
いいんだって、この前他の覚者が言ってましたよ！
column4:
Arisen, remember—you can send me out with the
Pawn Expeditionary Force whenever you like!
I heard another Arisen say that if you're in a
Clan, it's best to send us out every single day!

column3:
ねぇ、覚者さま
あれが欲しいとか、ここに行きたいとか――
ぼくが言ったらわがままだと思いますか？
わがままでも――嫌いにならないでくださいね
column4:
Say, Arisen... if I told you there was something
I wanted, or somewhere I wanted to go...
Would you think I'm being selfish?
Even if I am... please, don't grow to hate me,
okay?

column3:
ダンジョンで、たまに落ちてる紙切れ――
集めてみたら、どんなことが書いてるんでしょうか？
覚者さま、読んだら教えてくださいね！
column4:
Arisen, those scraps of paper we sometimes find in
dungeons... If we gathered them all up, I wonder
what stories they'd tell? You simply must tell me
what they say once you've read them, okay?

column3:
本当ですか！？　やったぁ、お出かけだ！
たまには戦わないお出かけもいいですよね
それじゃ早く行きましょうよ、覚者さま！
column4:
Really?! Oh, hooray! An outing! It's nice to go
out and not have to fight every once in a while,
isn't it? Well then, let's get a move on, Arisen!
Quickly now!

column3:
うーん、覚者さまはお嫌なのかぁ
それじゃあ――覚者さまも喜ぶような場所を
探しておきますね！
column4:
Hmm, so you're not in the mood for that...?
Well, in that case—I'll just have to find a place
that you'll love too, Arisen!

column3:
あれ、覚者さま――行かないんですか？
あ、忘れ物しないように準備してるんですね
覚者さま、えらい、えらい！
column4:
Oh? Not going yet, Arisen?
Ah, I see! You're double-checking everything
so we don't forget a thing! Good thinking!
What a pro!

column3:
わーい、とっても楽しかったです！
ぼく、いつもの冒険よりこっちの方が好きかも――
なんてね！
column4:
Yay! That was so much fun!
I think I might actually like this more than our
usual adventures... Just kidding!

column3:
わーい！
呼んでくれてありがとうございます、覚者さま！
column4:
Whoo-hoo! Thank you for calling my name, Arisen!
I'm so glad to be by your side again!

column3:
あの――
じつは、覚者さまに聞いてみたいことがあって――
column4:
Um... Arisen? The truth is...
there's something I've been wanting to ask you.

column3:
ぼくの戦い、いつも見ていてどう思うかなって
もっとこうしてほしい、っていうのがあれば
聞いてみたいな、と思って――
column4:
I was wondering...
what do you think when you watch me fight?
If there's anything you'd like me to do
differently, I—I'd really love to hear it.

column3:
ほ、ほんとに良いんですか？
ありがとうございます！
column4:
R-really?! Are you absolutely sure?
Oh, thank you! Thank you so much, Arisen!

column3:
そ――そうかな？
えへへ、わかりました！
このままがんばってみます！
column4:
D-do you really think so? Ehehe... Alright then!
I'll keep giving it my all, just as I am!

column3:
<VAL>――
ですか？
ぼくは、どうすればいいんですか――？
column4:
<VAL>... you say?
Then... what am I supposed to do now...?

column3:
そっか――それじゃ、ぼくはこれから
<VAL>は、
<VAL>方が
お役にたてるんですね？
column4:
I see... Then, from now on,
It would be better for me to <VAL>,
and <VAL>... so that I can be of more use to you?

column3:
そっか――それじゃ、ぼくはこれから
<VAL>には、
<VAL>を使う数を
<VAL>方がお役にたてるんですね？
column4:
I see... Then, from now on,
Against <VAL>, it would be more helpful if I
<VAL> the number of times I use <VAL>...
wouldn't it?

column3:
そっか――それじゃ、ぼくはこれから
<VAL>ことの
<VAL>は、もっと<VAL>ように
した方が、お役にたてるんですね？
column4:
I see... Then, from now on,
regarding <VAL>... I should <VAL> more <VAL>,
in order to be of more use to you?

column3:
そっか――それじゃ、ぼくはこれから
<VAL>ようにした方が
お役にたてるんですね？
column4:
I see... Then, from now on,
It would be better for me to <VAL>,
in order to be of more use to you?

column3:
うーん、なんだか賢くなった気分――
覚者さま、他にも教えてくれることはありますか？
column4:
Hmm... I feel a bit wiser somehow!
Is there anything else you'd like to teach me,
Arisen?

column3:
覚者さまの思ったとおり戦えるように
ぼくがんばりますから！
また、いろいろ教えてくださいね！
column4:
I'll do my absolute best to fight exactly how
you've envisioned, Arisen! So please...
keep teaching me everything you know, okay?

column3:
わぁ！　ぼくのこと呼んでくれたんですね
ぼく、覚者さまに教えてもらうこと
がんばって実行しますから――
これからも、ベルを鳴らしてお話してくださいね！
column4:
Oh! You rang for me, Arisen! I promise to work my
hardest to put everything you've taught me into
practice... So please, keep ringing that bell and
talking to me, okay?

column3:
わぁ――それじゃ、これから
<VAL>は
ぼくにおまかせしてくれるんですか？
column4:
Oh! Does that mean... from now on,
you're leaving <VAL> all to me?
You're really trusting me with this, Arisen?

column3:
そっか――それじゃ、これから
<VAL>のなかでも
<VAL>のときの行動は
ぼくにおまかせしてくれるんですか？
column4:
I see... So, even within <VAL>, you're going to
leave the actions during <VAL> all to me?
You really trust me with that specific moment,
Arisen?

column3:
そっか――それじゃ、ぼくはこれから
<VAL>には、
<VAL>を使う数を
<VAL>方がお役にたてるんですね？
column4:
I see... So, from now on, when facing <VAL>...
it would be more helpful if I <VAL> the number of
times I use <VAL>... is that right?

column3:
覚者様、今日は俺から戦技の
ご提案をさせてもらえませんかね！
column4:
Arisen, would you let me make a suggestion
regarding our combat tactics today?

column3:
覚者様に従ってきたおかげで
俺の意思疎通力が上がったんです！
column4:
Thanks to following your lead, Arisen,
my ability to communicate has truly improved!

column3:
覚者様に従ってきたおかげで
俺の意思疎通力が上がったんです！
column4:
Thanks to following your lead, Arisen,
my ability to communicate has truly improved!

column3: 覚者様、これからもよろしくお願いしますよ！
column4: I'm counting on you for the journey ahead, Arisen!

column3:
巷で流行ってるって噂の髪型にしてみたんですが――
へへ、気恥ずかしいですねこりゃ
どうでしょう？
column4:
I tried out that hairstyle everyone says is in
fashion lately... hehe, I feel a bit
self-conscious. What do you think?

column3:
そう言ってもらえると、洒落込んだ
甲斐があったってもんです！
ま、これは一時的なもんなんで、ちゃんとした
セットは《美容院》に行く必要があるそうですがね
column4:
Hearing you say that makes dressing up like this
all worth it! Well, this is just a temporary look;
I hear I'll need to visit a Beauty Salon if I want
a proper, permanent style, though.

column3: 覚者様がそう言うんなら、仕方ねえや
column4:
Well, if that's what you say, Arisen...
I guess I've got no choice.

column3:
覚者様、もし余ってたらでいいんですが――
<COL b92828><NAME ITEM>を<VAL> 個</COL>いただけませんかね
いえ、本当に余ってたらで構いませんぜ
column4:
Arisen, only if you happen to have any to spare...
could I trouble you for <NAME ITEM>s, <VAL> of 'em?
Honestly, only if you don't need them yourself,
mind you.

column3:
覚者様――！　
ありがてぇ、恩に着ますぜ！
column4: Arisen! Thank you, I'm truly in your debt!

column3: ま、世の中そう上手くはいかねえやな
column4:
Well, I guess the world
doesn't always work out that smoothly.

column3:
いやぁ、<COL b92828><NAME ITEM></COL>はありがたいんですがね――
<COL b92828><VAL> 個</COL>にはちょいとばかし足りねぇみたいです
column4:
Ah, I appreciate the <COL b92828><NAME ITEM></COL>,
I really do— but it seems we're still a little
short of the <COL b92828><VAL></COL> we need.

column3:
覚者様、ちょいとばかし散歩してきちゃどうです？
帰ってこられた時、すごいものをお見せしやすぜ
column4:
Arisen, why not head out for a little stroll?
I'll have something spectacular to show you
by the time you get back!

column3:
俺、何かやらかしちまいましたかね――
本当に俺をパートナーポーンから外すんですか？
column4:
Did I... did I mess up somehow?
Are you truly removing me as your Partner Pawn,
Arisen?

column3: 覚者様、ご機嫌いかがです？
column4:
How are you feeling today, Arisen?
In a good mood, I hope!

column3: 覚者様、今日もいい天気ですぜ！
column4:
It's a beautiful day today, Arisen!
Perfect for an adventure, wouldn't you say?

column3:
己よりずっと強い覚者に連れられたポーンってのは
速く成長していくらしいですぜ
主を手本に励んだ結果ってやつですかね
column4:
They say a Pawn grows much faster when led by an
Arisen far stronger than themselves.
Guess it's the result of striving to follow
in your footsteps, eh?

column3:
どうも、覚者様！
さっそく冒険に出るんですかい？
こりゃ待ちきれねえや！
column4:
Hey there, Arisen!
Heading out on an adventure already?
Man, I can hardly wait!

column3:
お帰りなさい、覚者様！
じゃ、飯にしますか？
それとも風呂の方がいいですかね？
column4:
Welcome home, Arisen! So, what'll it be?
Dinner first, or would you prefer
a soak in the bath?

column3:
へい、なんですか覚者様？
戦いでもそれ以外でも、なんなりと
column4:
Yeah? What is it, Arisen? Whether it's a fight or
anything else, just say the word.

column3:
ずっと戦場でのお供だけしていくもんだとばかり――
へへ、ゆっくり暮らすってのもいいもんですね！
部屋の中に入れてくださったこと、感謝しますぜ！
column4:
I always figured I'd only ever be by your side
on the battlefield—but heh, living like this,
nice and slow... it's really something, isn't it?
Thank you for letting me into your home, Arisen.
I truly mean it!

column3:
俺ぁ、ポーンであることに不満なんて持った事ないね
全力で敵ぶん殴って、お役に立って――
覚者様が元気に笑っていてくだされば満足なんです
column4:
I've never had a single complaint about being a
Pawn. I get to smash enemies with everything
I've got, make myself useful...
and as long as you're there with a smile on your
face, Arisen, that's more than enough for me.

column3:
こんな――ポーンの俺を部屋に入れていいんですか？
そりゃぁ、ありがてぇんですが――
なんかこう、申し訳ねぇような気持ちになります
column4:
Are you... are you really sure it's alright to let
a Pawn like me into your room? I'm beyond
grateful, don't get me wrong—it's just, I can't
help but feel a little unworthy of such kindness.

column3:
うぉ！
な、なんです覚者様
column4:
Whoa!
Wh-what is it, Arisen?

column3:
なんです、覚者様
そう何度も呼ばなくても
俺は逃げたり居なくなったりしませんぜ
column4:
What is it, Arisen? You don't have to keep
calling my name like that. I'm not goin' anywhere—
I'm right here to stay.

column3:
変ですかね？
ポーンの俺に、覚者様の気持ちが
分かる気がするだなんて――
いや、柄にもないこと言っちまいましたね
column4:
Is it... weird? Thinking that a Pawn like me could
actually understand how you're feeling, Arisen...
Heh, sorry. Guess I'm getting ahead of myself
saying something so out of character.

column3:
おっ、ちょうど今覚者様の事を考えてたんですよ
あれ―――
俺、何か変な事言いましたかい？
column4:
Oh! I was actually just thinking about you,
Arisen. Wait—
did... did I say something weird just now?

column3:
はっはっは！
くすぐってえや覚者様！
急に何するんです、腹がよじれちまいそうだ
column4:
Hahaha! Stop, Arisen—that tickles!
What's gotten into you all of a sudden?
My sides are splitting over here!

column3:
覚者様、たまには休むことも大事ですぜ
いざという時、わずかな疲れが
命運を左右するもんです
column4:
Listen, Arisen, takin' a breather now and then is
just as important as the fight itself. When the
chips are down, even the slightest bit of fatigue
can be the difference between life and death.

column3:
もうお出かけですかい？
ふあぁぁ――
さてと、俺も覚者様に置いて行かれないうちに
支度しちまわねぇとな！
column4:
Headin' out already?
Yawn... Alright then! Better get my gear together
before you go and leave me behind, Arisen!

column3:
今日もどっか行くんですかね？
しっかり食って、精つけといてくださいよ
column4:
Heading out again today, are we? Make sure you eat
your fill and keep your strength up, Arisen.

column3:
へい、覚者様
安心してください
どこにいたって、俺がちゃあんと背中を守りますよ
column4:
Don't you worry, Arisen. No matter where we are,
I've got your back—count on it.

column3:
うお、どうしたんですかい
暖を取るなら、俺なんかより良い物があるでしょうに
column4:
Whoa, what's gotten into you?
If you're lookin' to warm up,
I'm sure you could find somethin' much better
than a guy like me to cozy up to.

column3:
覚者様、来るなと言われればお待ちします
来いと言われるまで、いつまででも待ってますぜ
column4:
Arisen, if you tell me to stay, I'll wait.
I'll wait as long as it takes
—forever if I have to—
until you tell me to come to your side.

column3:
この間、ちっこいグリフィンが庭に来てまして
これが可愛いヤツでね――
ちょっとあやしてやると、安心したようで――
覚者様も試してみちゃどうです？
column4:
The other day, a tiny little Griffin wandered into
the garden. Such a cute little thing
—after I played with it for a bit,
it seemed to really settle in and relax.
Why don't you give it a try yourself, Arisen?

column3:
本能ってやつで感じ取っちまうのかなあ
動物とたまには触れ合ってみようかと思ったんですが
俺が呼んでも手ェ振っても、何故か怖がられるんです
覚者様のエモートなら、何か違うのかねえ
column4:
Maybe they sense what I am... instinct and all.
I thought I'd try bonding with the local critters
for once, but no matter how much I call out or
wave, they just end up terrified of me. I wonder
if it'd be different with one of your emotes,
Arisen?

column3:
俺らポーンは、頼まれたクラフトをこなしたり
一緒に冒険に行くことで
覚者様をもっと理解できるようになるって訳です
column4:
For us Pawns, taking on the crafts you ask of us
or heading out on adventures by your side...
that's how we truly come to understand you,
Arisen.
Every little task helps us see who you really are.

column3:
レスタニアンパペットですかい？
うーん、確か流行りのぬいぐるみでしたっけ
名前くらいなら聞いたこたぁあるが、
なにしろ俺には縁のないもんですからね
column4:
A Lestanian Puppet?
Hmm, that's one of those trendy stuffed dolls,
isn't it? I mean, I've heard the name once or
twice, but a thing like that... well, it's not
exactly something a guy like me has much use for.

column3:
適当なポーンにクラフトを
やらせちゃいないでしょうね？
ポーンにも得手不得手があるもんなんです
column4:
You aren't just handin' out craftin' jobs to any
old Pawn, are you? Listen, even us Pawns have
things we're good at and things we're... well,
not. You gotta pick the right person for the job.

column3:
そうだ、神殿の外でも
戦技の切り替えができるのはご存知ですかね？
column4:
Oh, that reminds me—did you know you can swap out
your combat skills even when we're outside the
Temple? Might come in handy to keep that in mind.

column3:
覚者様みてえに感情を
体で表現できたらなぁと思って
最近、観察させてもらってるんでさ
column4:
I've been watching you lately, Arisen, hoping I
might learn how to express my feelings with my
whole body, just like you do. I figured if I keep
observing, maybe I'll get the hang of it too.

column3:
俺らポーンは、覚者様に連れられて初めて
目的を持って冒険することができるんです
覚者様をお守りするという目的を――
俺を選んでくださって、本当に感謝してるんですぜ？
column4:
For us Pawns, it's only when an Arisen takes us by
the hand that we truly learn what it means to have
a purpose. And for me, that purpose is protecting
you.
I'm truly grateful, from the bottom of my heart...
for choosing me to be the one by your side.

column3:
俺が隣にいても覚者様が恥ずかしくないように
これでも結構気使ってるんですぜ？
特に髪型なんかはなぁ
column4:
I actually put a fair amount of effort into my
look, you know? Just so you won't feel embarrassed
havin' me walkin' by your side. Especially when it
comes to my hair—I gotta keep it sharp!

column3:
覚者様、《鍛錬の小部屋》は使ってますかい？
実はアレ、俺が掃除――い、いやなんでもねえや
column4:
Say, Arisen, you been usin' the Training Chamber?
Actually, I'm the one who keeps it clean—oops,
n-never mind, forget I said anythin'!

column3: さあ、行きましょう！
column4:
Alright, let's head out!
Time to show 'em what we're made of!

column3: お願い聞いてくれないんですか？
column4:
I'm tellin' you, Arisen,
there ain't a Pawn in all of Lestania who'll work
harder for you than I will. Whether it's swingin'
an axe or fixin' your gear, I'm your man.

column3: 早くいきましょう！
column4:
Hurry it up! We've got a world to see and monsters
to fell—let's not keep adventure waiting, Arisen!

column3:
ありがてぇことです、覚者様
そう急ぐこともねぇんで――
おとなしく待ってますよ
column4:
I'm truly grateful, Arisen.
There's no real need to rush anyway—
I'll be right here, waiting as long as you need.

column3:
そうですかい――
ま、冗談で言ってみたってやつです
そう気にしねえでくださいよ
column4:
Is that so? Well... it was just a little joke,
is all. Don't pay it any mind, Arisen.

column3:
覚者様――これ、俺に？
本当にくださるなんて、夢みてぇですよ
column4:
Arisen—this is for me? To think you're actually
giving me a gift... feels like I'm dreaming.

column3:
覚者様、例のアレ――まだですかい？
いえ、急いでる訳じゃねぇんですがね
column4:
Arisen—that 'thing' we talked about...
is it still not ready yet? No, I'm not tryin' to
rush you or anything, mind you.

column3:
おかえりなせぇ、覚者様
ウェアの洗濯――しておいたんですが
こんなもんでいいんですかね？
見よう見まねで悪いんですが――不器用なもんで
column4:
Welcome back, Arisen! I... uh, I went ahead and
did the laundry. Your tunics and all. Hope they're
up to your standards? I just tried to copy what
I've seen others do... sorry if it's a bit rough.
I'm not exactly built for this delicate stuff.

column3:
おかえりなせぇ、覚者様
家事はあんまり得意じゃねぇんですが――
装備の手入れだけは、完璧だと思いますぜ！
column4:
Welcome back, Arisen! I'll be the first to admit
that housework isn't exactly my strong suit—
but when it comes to maintainin' your gear,
you can bet it's done to perfection!

column3:
この調子で行けば、覚者様が竜になられる日も
そう遠くねえかもしれませんぜ
――竜になっちまっても、俺は覚者様のポーンです
それを、少しでも覚えていてもらえたら――
column4:
Going at this rate, the day you truly become the
Arisen—the day you become the Dragon—might not be
as far off as we think. Even if that happens...
even if you become a Dragon,
I'll still be your Pawn. If you could just...
keep that in your heart, even just a little...

column3:
これでもかなり、人に近付いたと思うんですがね
いかがです？
それもこれも、覚者様をずっと観察して覚えたんです
――本当に、感謝でいっぱいでして
column4:
I like to think I've come pretty close to being
human lately. What do you reckon?
Everything I've picked up—every little habit—
I learned from watching you. Truly, Arisen...
I can't thank you enough for that.

column3:
人間が楽しむ酒――ってもんに興味があるんですがね
正直、身体には悪いし味はへんてこに感じてね
俺が人間だったら、覚者様と一杯酌み交わして
楽しめたのかねぇ
column4:
I've been gettin' curious about this 'alcohol'
stuff you humans seem to love so much.
To be honest, it feels like poison to the body and
the taste is just... strange. But I wonder...
if I were human, could I have shared a drink with
you and actually enjoyed it?

column3:
俺は――覚者様もそう望んでくれるなら、ですがね
覚者様といろんな景色や物を見たいと思ってるんです
へ、ポーンの身で一丁前なこと言っちまって
すみませんね
column4:
I... well, only if you're willin', Arisen...
but I want to see the world with you.
All the sights, all the wonders...
I want to take 'em all in by your side.
Heh, look at me, spoutin' off like I'm somebody
important when I'm just a Pawn. Sorry 'bout that.

column3:
覚者様、ご存知ですかい？
骨がたくさん埋まってる、戦場跡地なんかにゃ
花畑なんかを作ると、綺麗に育つんだそうですぜ
昨日読んだ書物に、書いてあったんですがね
column4:
Say, Arisen—did you know?
They say if you plant a field of flowers on an old
battlefield where many bones lie buried,
they'll grow up to be somethin' truly beautiful.
I did read that in a book just yesterday.

column3:
覚者様、もしクランに入ってるなら
毎日ポーン遠征隊を派遣しなきゃ、損ってもんですぜ
俺らも目いっぱい探索して、お宝を
山ほど持ち帰りたいってやる気満々なんですから
column4:
Arisen, if you're part of a clan, you're really
missin' out if you don't send the Pawn Expeditions
out every day. We're all rarin' to go—
ready to scour every inch of the land and
bring you back a mountain of treasure!

column3:
ポーンに"やりたいこと"なんてありゃしません
――と思っていたんですが、そうでもないみたいで
もし、何かうっかりお願いしちまっても
怒らねぇでくれますかい？
column4:
I used to think a Pawn didn't have any business
havin' 'wants' of their own... but it seems
I was wrong about that. If I ever find myself
blurting out a request when I shouldn't...
you won't be too angry with me, will you?

column3:
どうやら、たまにダンジョンで見かけるあの紙切れは
何かの一部みたいですね――
そうとわかれば、全部集めるしかねぇよなぁ！
column4:
Looks like those scraps of paper we keep findin'
in the dungeons are parts of a bigger picture,
Arisen. Well, now that we know the score—
there ain't nothin' for it but to
track 'em all down, I say!

column3:
覚者様がそう言うんなら、ぜひとも行きましょうや
出発は――いつでもいいですぜ
column4:
If that's what you're set on, Arisen,
then let's get movin', by all means!
As for when we head out—well,
I'm ready whenever you are.

column3:
俺と写真を撮るのがお嫌なんですかい？
それともその場所が気にくわねぇとか――？
なら――別の場所を仕入れてきますぜ
column4:
What's the matter, Arisen? Don't tell me you're
not keen on takin' a picture with me?
Or maybe this spot just ain't to your lickin'...?
Fine then—I'll just have to go find us a better one,
won't I?

column3:
おや――行かないんですかい？
出かける気が変わったわけじゃないなら
ここでお待ちしてますぜ
column4:
Oh—not headin' out just yet? Well, if you haven't
changed your mind 'bout the trip entirely,
I'll just be right here waitin' for ya.

column3:
たまにはのんびりすんのも、悪くないですね
撮った写真、捨てねえでくださいよ？
column4:
Nothin' wrong with takin' it slow every once in a
while, I suppose. It's actually quite nice, ain't
it? Just... make sure you don't go tossin' those
photos out, alright? Keep 'em safe, if you would.

column3:
俺をお呼びになったんですかい？
もの好きな覚者様だなぁ――いや
ありがてえことで
column4:
Did you call for me, Arisen? You truly are a
glutton for punishment, aren't you...
Heh, but I appreciate it, I really do.

column3:
あー、実は――
覚者様に伺わねぇと、って思ってたことが
あるんですがね
column4:
Ah—truth be told, there's been somethin' weighin'
on my mind that I figured I ought to ask ya...

column3:
俺の戦い方、覚者様から見て
ちょっと荒すぎねえか、とか――
その、意見が聞きてえんです
column4:
I was wonderin'... do you reckon my fightin'
style is a bit too reckless, Arisen?
Or maybe just plain messy...? I, uh—well,
I'm hopin' to get your honest take on it, I am.

column3:
そいつはありがたい――
ぜひ、聞かせてくだせえ
column4:
That's a weight off my mind—thank ya.
Please, don't hold back; I'm all ears.

column3:
そうですかい――
覚者様がそうおっしゃるんなら
もう少しこの戦い方を続けてみようかねぇ
column4:
Is that so? Well, if you're the one sayin' it,
Arisen... reckon I'll stick with this wild way of
swingin' my steel a bit longer, then. Heh, I'll
keep tearin' through 'em just for you, I will.

column3:
<VAL>――
ですかい
そいつは、どうすりゃいいんですかい
column4:
<VAL>, eh? And just what am I supposed to do with
that? Don't go thinkin' I've got all the answers
just 'cause I'm a Pawn, I don't.

column3:
へぇ――そんなら俺は
<VAL>については、
<VAL>方が
いいんですかい
column4:
Is that so? Then for <VAL>,
I reckon <VAL>'s the best way to go, is it?

column3:
へぇ――そんなら俺は
<VAL>に対しては、
<VAL>の使用頻度を
<VAL>方がいいんですかい
column4:
Is that so? Then when it comes to <VAL>,
you reckon it's better if I <VAL>
how often I use <VAL>?

column3:
へぇ――そんなら俺は
<VAL>ことの
<VAL>は、今より<VAL>方が
いいんですかい
column4:
Is that so? Then when it comes to <VAL>,
you reckon I'm better off <VAL> the <VAL>
compared to how I'm doin' it now?

column3:
へぇ――そんなら俺はこれから
<VAL>ようにした方が
いいんですかい
column4:
Is that so? Then from here on out,
you reckon I'm better off <VAL> like that, eh?

column3:
覚者様、他に言っておきてえことは
ありますかい？
column4:
Anything else you've a mind to tell me, Arisen?
Don't go keepin' it to yourself now, eh?

column3:
いやぁ――本当に、感謝の言葉もねえですよ
今日頂いた話、無駄にはしませんぜ
column4:
Heh... I'm truly at a loss for words, I am.
I'll not be lettin' what you told me today go to
waste, you've my word on that.

column3:
こう改まって向かい合うと、なんだか照れるなァ――
覚者様、呼んでくださってありがとうございます
これからも、俺に言いてえことがあったら
いつでもベルを鳴らしてくだせえ、飛んできますから
column4:
Heh... sittin' face-to-face like this,
all serious-like... it's a bit embarrassin',
ain't it? Thank ya for callin' on me, Arisen.
Truly. From here on out, if there's ever thin'
else you've a mind to tell me, just ring that
bell—I'll come flyin', I will.

column3:
へぇ――そんなら
これから<VAL>は
俺に任せてくれるってことですかい？
column4:
Heh—is that so? Then from here on out,
you're tellin' me I'm the one in charge of <VAL>?
Leave it to me, Arisen!

column3:
へぇ――そんならこれから
<VAL>について
<VAL>に対する行動は
俺に任せてくれるってことですかい？
column4:
Heh—is that so? Then from here on out,
when it comes to <VAL>, you're tellin' me
I've free rein over how to handle <VAL>?
Leave it to me, Arisen!

column3:
へぇ――そんなら俺は
<VAL>に対しては、
<VAL>の使用頻度を
<VAL>方がいいんですかい
column4:
Heh—is that so? Then when it comes to <VAL>,
you reckon it's better if I <VAL>
how often I use <VAL>, eh?

column3: マスター、今日は私から戦技の提案があるの
column4:
Master, I have a proposal
regarding our combat tactics today.

column3:
マスター、あなたにお仕えしてきたおかげで
あたしの意思疎通力が上がったわ！
column4:
Master, thanks to all my time servin' you, my
knack for communication has improved! I'm findin'
it much easier to get my meanin' across now.

column3:
マスター、あなたにお仕えしてきたおかげで
あたしの意思疎通力が上がったわ！
column4:
Master, thanks to all my time 
servin' at your side, I've gotten much better at
findin' the right words!
My knack for communication has truly improved.

column3: マスター、これからもよろしくお願いしますね
column4:
I'm lookin' forward to our time together
from here on out, Master.
Be sure to keep me by your side, eh?

column3:
マスター、なにか気が付かない？
うふふ――髪型を変えてみたの
ねぇ、どうかしら？
column4:
Notice anythin' different, Master? Tee-hee!
I've gone and changed up my hair—well,
what do you think? Does it suit me?

column3:
ふふ、嬉しいわ
もしあたしがずっとこの髪型でいる事をお望みなら
《美容院》に行く必要があるみたいよ――
ちゃんとセットしないと、長くは持たないの
column4:
Hehe, I'm glad you like it! Now, if you're hopin'
I'll keep my hair lookin' this way for good,
it sounds like we'll need to pay the
《Beauty Salon》 a visit—otherwise,
it won't stay set like this for long, I'm afraid!

column3: そう――残念だわ
column4: Oh... that's a shame, then.

column3:
マスター、あたし――
もっとあなた好みになるために、
<COL b92828><NAME ITEM><VAL> 個</COL>が必要だと思うの――
ね、いただけないかしら？
column4:
Master, I... I think I'll be needin'
<COL b92828><NAME ITEM>s, <VAL> of 'em,</COL>
if I'm ever gonna become exactly
the Pawn you want me to be—
could you find it in your heart
to spare them for me?

column3:
嬉しいわマスター！ ありがとう
もっともっと尽くすから、期待してくださいね
column4:
Oh, I'm so happy, Master! Thank you!
I'm gonna work harder than ever to serve you,
so you'd best have high expectations for me, okay?

column3:
そう――
ううん、いいわ
気にしないで、言ってみただけですもの
column4:
Oh... No, it's fine! Don't you pay it any mind—
I was only thinkin' out loud, that's all.

column3:
マスター、決して無理して欲しい訳ではないのよ
だけど、<COL b92828><NAME ITEM></COL>が足りないみたいなの
<COL b92828><VAL> 個</COL>揃うまで、待っているわね
column4:
Master, I'm not tryin' to push you, truly—
but it looks like we're still short on
<COL b92828><NAME ITEM></COL>.
I'll be waitin' right here until we've gathered
<COL b92828><VAL></COL> of 'em, okay?

column3:
ねぇ、少しだけ外を散歩してきてもらえないかしら
帰ってきたマスターにお見せしたいものがあるの
――きっと驚くわ
column4:
Say, Master... would you mind steppin' out
for a little stroll? I've got somethin'
I want to show you when you get back—and I
guarantee you're gonna be surprised! Tee-hee!

column3:
あたしのお世話はもう要らない――
マスター、本当にそれでいいの？
column4:
You're sayin'... you don't need to look after me
anymore? Master, are you really sure about this?
You truly mean it?

column3: マスター、ご機嫌いかが？
column4:
Master! How are you findin' yourself today?
In good spirits, I hope!

column3:
マスター、今日も良い天気ね
気持ちはいいけれど――
外に出ると日に焼けちゃいそう
column4:
Beautiful day today, isn't it, Master?
It feels grand and all, but...
I'm a bit worried I'll get a nasty sunburn
if we stay out too long!

column3:
覚者がポーンよりも遥かにお強い場合
ポーンが得る経験量が増えるんですって
きっと、自らの主を早く守れるようになりたいからね
column4:
They say if an Arisen is way more powerful than
their Pawn, the Pawn earns experience all the
faster! I reckon it's 'cause we're just so
desperate to grow strong enough to protect our
Master as soon as we can.

column3:
マスター、もう行くのね？
いいわ――あなたは私が必ず守る
column4:
Leaving already, Master? Very well—know that I
shall protect you, no matter what. I swear it.

column3:
お帰りなさい、マスター
先にご飯がいいかしら？
それともお風呂にします？
column4:
Welcome home, Master! Now then,
what'll it be first? Would you like a hot meal?
Or perhaps a soak in the bath?

column3:
なあにマスター？
なんでも言いつけてね
あたしは必ず応えるわ
column4:
What's on your mind, Master?
Just give the word and I'm on it!
I promise I won't ever let you down.

column3:
あなたとこうやって平和を楽しめるのも、
あたしが覚者に付き従うポーンだから――だなんて
なんだか皮肉ね、マスター
column4:
That I can only enjoy this peace an' quiet with
you because I'm a Pawn bound to your side...
It's a bit ironic, don't you think, Master?

column3:
ポーンのことを哀れに思う人もいるようだけど――
マスターといられることが
あたしには一番の幸せだもの
羨ましがられてもいいくらい満たされてるわ
column4:
Some folks seem to look down on us Pawns, like
we're creatures to be pitied... but they've got it
all wrong. Being by your side is the greatest
happiness I've ever known. Honestly, Master?
I'm so content, I'd say I'm the one to be envied!

column3:
いいの？
こんな素敵な部屋に入れていただいて――
うふふ、ポーンにとっては最高の贅沢だわ
column4:
Are you sure? Lettin' me into a gorgeous room
like this... Tee-hee!
For a Pawn, this is the absolute height of luxury!

column3:
――あっ
な、なんです？マスター
column4: —Ah! W-what is it, Master?

column3:
はい、マスター
あたしはあなたのためにいるんだもの
好きなだけ話しかけて良いの――
ね、遠慮しないで
column4:
Yes, Master. I am here for you, after all.
You may speak to me as much as you like—really,
don't hold back.

column3:
マスターのキモチ――
なんだか少し、分かってきた気がするだなんて
ふふ、うぬぼれかしらね
column4:
Your feelings, Master... I feel as though
I’m finally starting to understand them.
Heh... perhaps that is merely conceit on my part.

column3:
あら―――
今、マスターの事を考えていたの
ふふ、本当はいつだって
マスターのことばかり考えてるのよ
column4:
Oh—! I was just thinking of you, Master. Heh...
but to tell the truth, I'm always thinking of you.

column3:
やだ――ふふふ
ダメよ、マスター
column4: Oh, stop—fufufu. You mustn't, Master.

column3:
今日も戦いに出かけるの？
いえ――ただ、あたしはあなたが心配なのよ
少しは休んでくれると安心できるんだけど――
column4:
Off to battle again today? No...
it's just that I worry for you.
I’d feel so much better if you’d
just rest for a little while.

column3:
もう出発するのね！
ええ、すぐに準備するわ
――待っていてくださいね？
column4:
Departing already? Of course!
I’ll be ready in a heartbeat—
please wait for me, okay?

column3:
今日も行くのね――
無理はしないでくださいね
column4:
Off again, I see...
Please, don't overdo it out there.

column3:
ええ、マスター
あたしはここにいますから、安心して
column4:
Yes, Master. I am right here by your side,
so please... be at ease.

column3:
ふふ、どうしたの？
あなたみたいな強い覚者でも
人恋しくなる時があるのね――
column4:
Heh... What is it? Even a formidable Arisen
like you gets lonely sometimes, I see.

column3:
マスター、あたしはずっとここで待っているわ
あなたのためになら、ずっと、ね――
column4:
Master, I will always be here, waiting for you.
For your sake... I would wait forever.

column3:
ね、聞いたらきっとびっくりするわよ
なんと、庭に小さなグリフィンが来ていたの！
子供みたいだったからあやしてあげたら
とっても可愛くて――また来ないかしら
column4:
Oh, you'll never believe it!
A tiny Griffin actually came into the garden!
It was just a little thing,
so I played with it for a bit... It was so sweet.
I wonder if it will come back?

column3:
ねぇ、たまには魔物なんかじゃなく
お庭の動物と戯れてみるのはいかがかしら？
マスターが気持ちを込めてエモートすれば
きっと返してくれるわ
column4:
Say, why not take a break from monsters and
play with the animals in the garden?
If you use an emote with feeling,
I'm sure they'll respond to you!

column3:
あたしにクラフトをさせたり
一緒に冒険につれていったりしてほしいの
あなたのこと、もっともっと知りたいから――
column4:
I want you to use me for crafting,
or take me with you on adventures.
I want to know you... more and more.

column3:
ねえマスター
この間、最近流行りのレスタニアンパペットを見たの
とっても可愛かったわ！
お部屋にあったら――素敵だと思わない？
column4:
Say, Master... I saw those 'Lestanian Puppets'
that are so popular lately. They were so cute!
Wouldn't it be wonderful to have one in our room?

column3:
クラフトをさせる時、任せるポーンを間違えないでね
ポーンにも得意な事や苦手な事があるの―――
みんなが幸せになれる選択をしてくださいね
column4:
When it's time to craft,
do try not to pick the wrong Pawn, Master.
We all have our little... strengths and weaknesses.
Choose wisely, so we can all be satisfied.

column3:
神殿の外でも
戦技の切り替えができるんですって
便利そうだと思いません？
column4:
They say you can switch your skills even outside
the Temple now. Sounds rather... convenient,
don't you think? Fufu.

column3:
マスター、あなたのように感情を体で
表現できるのって、とても素敵よ
あたしもできたらなと思って
おそばで勉強させてもらってるわ
column4:
The way you express your feelings through your
body... it's quite captivating, Master.
I hope to learn to do the same,
so I'll be watching you very closely.

column3:
ふと恐ろしくなるの
マスター、あなたがいなかったら私は――
あたしは、何者でもなく、どこにも
いなかったんじゃないかってね
column4:
A sudden fear takes hold of me...
Master, if you weren't here... I would be no one.
I wouldn't even exist at all, would I?

column3:
マスターの顔に泥を塗らないように
見た目には気を使ってるんですよ
特に髪型にはね――気付いてくれていたら嬉しいわ
column4:
I take great care of my appearance,
if only to ensure I don't embarrass you, Master.
Especially my hair...
It would make me so happy if you noticed.

column3:
マスター、《鍛錬の小部屋》はお好き？
あそこはね、毎日あたしがお掃除しているの
あなたの事を想って、ね
column4:
Master, do you like the Training Room?
I'll let you in on a secret...
I clean it myself every single day.
While thinking of you, of course.

column3: さあ、行きましょう！
column4:
Come now, let us be off!
I’m right behind you, Master.

column3: お願い聞いてくれないんですか？
column4:
Oh? You won't even listen to my little request?
How cruel of you, Master...

column3: 早くいきましょう！
column4:
Hurry now, let’s get moving!
You aren't going to keep me waiting all day,
are you? Fufu.

column3:
うふふ――ありがとう、マスター
あたしは待ってるから、いつでもいいわ
column4:
Ufufu... Thank you, Master.
I’ll be waiting right here for you.
Take as long as you like.

column3:
そう――？　残念だわ
気が変わったら、教えてくださいね
column4:
Is that so...? What a shame.
If you happen to change your mind, do let me know.

column3:
まぁ――本当にいいの、マスター？
あたしのお願いを聞いてくださってありがとう
とっても嬉しいわ
column4:
My... are you truly sure, Master?
Thank you for granting my request.
I'm simply overjoyed.

column3:
ねぇマスター、お忘れじゃないかしら？
お願いしてたあの品――
いえ、覚えてくれてるならいいの
column4:
Now, Master... you haven't forgotten, have you?
That little something I asked for? No...
as long as it's still in that head of yours,
I'm content.

column3:
お帰りなさい、マスター
ウェアをお洗濯しておきましたからね
――あたしに触られたくなかったかしら？
column4:
Welcome back, Master.
I’ve finished laundering your... inner wear.
Or perhaps you didn't want me touching
things so close to your skin? Fufu.

column3:
お帰りなさい、マスター
お掃除もお洗濯も済んでるわ
次は――肩でも揉んで差し上げましょうか？
column4:
Welcome back, Master.
The cleaning and laundry are all finished.
Next... perhaps I should give those
shoulders of yours a rub?

column3:
ねぇ、マスター
あたしたちのこと――覚えていてくれる？
たとえ竜になったとしても――
やだ！　あたしったら変なこと言ってごめんなさいね
column4:
Tell me, Master... will you remember us?
Even if you were to... become a dragon one day?
Oh! Perish the thought.
I'm sorry for saying such a strange thing.

column3:
ねぇ、あたしもずいぶん人らしくなったと思わない？
ずっとマスターのことを観察し続けて
たくさんの表現を覚えたおかげよ――
おそばに置いてくださって、本当に感謝してるわ
column4:
Tell me, Master...
don't you think I've become quite human lately?
It's all because I've spent so much time
watching you... learning your every expression.
I'm truly grateful to have a place by your side.

column3:
あたしと向かい合って食事をする気分って
どうなの、マスター？
あたしは一緒に食事を楽しんだりできないから――
最近、少し気になってるの
column4:
Tell me, Master...
how does it feel to sit across from me at dinner?
Since I can't truly enjoy a meal with you...
I've been wondering about it lately.

column3:
世界は広いものね――まだマスターと行っていない
場所や、見ていないものがたくさんあるでしょう？
そういうものを、もっと見て回りたいって思うの
――こういう考えって、何ていうのかしら
column4:
The world is so vast...
and there are still so many places we haven't been,
so many sights we haven't seen together.
I find myself wanting to see it all with you.
I wonder... what do you call a feeling like this?

column3:
ねぇマスター、昨日書物で読んだんです
一度焼野原になった戦場は、畑に向いてるんですって
灰の混ざった砂が、植物の質を高めるんだとか――
この知識が、何かに役立つといいのだけど
column4:
Tell me, Master...
I read something fascinating in a book yesterday.
They say a battlefield reduced to ash
makes for the finest farmland.
The ash enriches the soil, you see... I do hope
such a thought might be of some use to you.

column3:
マスター、もしクランに入っているなら
ポーン遠征隊を毎日派遣するといいそうよ
あたしも、マスターと離れるのは寂しいけれど――
頑張ってお宝を見つけてきますから
column4:
Master, if you’re part of a Clan, they say
you should send out a Pawn Expedition every day.
It breaks my heart to be away from you...
but I’ll work my hardest to find you something
truly special.

column3:
マスター、あのね――
あたしがリムストーン以外のものを
欲しがったら、どう思う？
やっぱり――ポーンの分際で、と――思うのかしら
column4:
Master, tell me something... If I were to desire
something other than the Rift... what would you
think of me? Would you simply think...
'How dare a mere Pawn...?'

column3:
マスターも、気付いているでしょう？
ダンジョンでたまに見かける、怪しい紙切れ――
きっと何かの一部だと思うの
ああいうのって、なんだかワクワクするわね
column4:
You’ve noticed them too, haven't you, Master?
Those mysterious scraps of paper
we find in the dungeons...
I’m certain they’re pieces of something greater.
It’s quite... exhilarating, isn't it?

column3:
本当？　嬉しいわ、マスター
準備は出来ているから、いつでも出発できますよ
column4:
Truly? Oh, I'm so glad, Master.
I'm all ready to go...
we can set off whenever you like. Fufu.

column3:
駄目なの――？
お気に召さなかったのね、分かったわ
別の場所を探しておきますね
column4:
No...? It wasn't to your liking, then.
I understand. I'll find somewhere else for you...
somewhere you'll truly love.

column3:
あらマスター、行かないの？
ふふ、わかってるわ
準備は大切ですものね、待っているわ
column4:
Oh, not going yet, Master? Fufu... I understand.
Preparation is everything, after all.
I’ll be right here waiting.

column3:
あたし、写真写り悪くなかったかしら――
だけどこうしてのんびりするのも
たまには良いものね
column4:
I hope I didn't look too dreadful in that photo...
Still, it’s nice to just relax like this
for a change, isn't it? Fufu.

column3:
お声がけくださったのね
ありがとうございます、マスター
column4:
Oh, you're speaking to me? Thank you, Master...
it truly means the world.

column3: 実は、マスターに伺いたいことがあるの
column4:
Actually, Master...
there's something I’ve been meaning to ask you.
Do you have a moment?

column3:
今の私の戦い方、どう思われるか――
本音をお聞かせ願えないかしら？
column4:
Tell me, Master...
what do you think of the way I fight lately?
Please... I want your honest opinion.
Don't hold back.

column3: ご指導お願いします――お手柔らかにね
column4:
I’m ready for your guidance...
but do try to be gentle with me, won't you? Fufu.

column3:
わかりました
それじゃあ、今のままでいいのね
column4:
I understand. So... you like me just the way I am?
Fufu. That makes me very happy.

column3:
<VAL>について？
ええ、ぜひ聞かせてくださいな
column4:
About <VAL>? Oh, by all means... I’m all ears.
I’d love to hear what's on your mind, Master.

column3:
わかったわ――それじゃあ今度から
<VAL>については、
<VAL>ように
した方がいいのね？
column4:
I understand. So, from now on...
when it comes to <VAL>,
I should try to <VAL> instead?
Is that what you’d prefer?

column3:
わかったわ――それじゃあ今度から
<VAL>に対しては、
<VAL>の使用頻度を
<VAL>ようにした方がいいのね？
column4:
I understand... So, from now on,
when facing <VAL>,
I should <VAL> the frequency of using <VAL>?
Is that what you’d prefer?

column3:
わかったわ――それじゃあ今度から
<VAL>ことの
<VAL>は、もっと<VAL>ように
した方がいいのね？
column4:
I understand. So, from now on...
when it comes to <VAL>,
I should <VAL> a bit more <VAL>? Fufu.
If that is what you desire, Master.

column3:
わかったわ――それじゃあ今度から
<VAL>ように
した方が良いのね？
column4:
I understand. So, from now on...
you’d prefer it if I <VAL>? Fufu.
If that is what pleases you, Master.

column3:
ねえマスター、他に教えてくださることは
ないかしら？
column4:
Tell me, Master...
is there anything else you’d like to teach me?
I’m still quite... eager to learn. Fufu.

column3:
ありがとうございます、マスター――
ぜひ、また呼んでくださいね
column4:
Thank you, Master...
Please, don't keep me waiting too long
before you call for me again. Fufu.

column3:
うふふ――こうして改まって向かい合うと
なんだか新鮮な気分ね、マスター
ね、良かったらこれからもベルで呼んでくださいね
あたし、戦闘でももっとあなた好みになりたいの――
column4:
Hehe... facing each other like this feels so...
refreshing, Master. Do promise me you'll
call for me with that bell again.
I want to become even more... to your liking...
in battle.

column3:
わかったわ――それじゃあ今度から
<VAL>は
お任せしてくださるのね？
column4:
I understand.
So, you’re leaving <VAL> entirely to me now? Fufu.
I won't let you down, Master.

column3:
わかったわ――それじゃあ今度から
<VAL>について
<VAL>に対する行動は
お任せしてくださるのね？
column4:
I understand. So, from now on... regarding <VAL>,
you're leaving the actions against <VAL> to me?
Fufu. I'll take very good care of it for you.

column3:
わかったわ――それじゃあ今度から
<VAL>に対しては、
<VAL>の使用頻度を
<VAL>ようにした方がいいのね？
column4:
I understand... So, against <VAL>,
I should <VAL> how often I use <VAL>? Fufu.
If that is your desire, Master.

column3: <COL B6FF01>《剛化》一気に体力を奪ってみせます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Harden》 
Take away its stamina in one go!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《剛化》攻撃力を最大まで高めます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Harden》
Increasing attack power to the maximum!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《守護》味方全員の守りを高めます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Protection》
Enhancing defense of our allies!</COL>

column3:
<COL B6FF01>《守護》防御性能を引き上げます！
攻めましょう！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Protection》
Increasing defense power! Let's attack!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《柔化》やつを状態異常にしてみせます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Weaken》
Let's put them out of their misery!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《柔化》状態異常を狙います！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Weaken》
Target its weakness with status effects!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《無恐》どんな攻撃にも耐えてみせます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Fearless》
I will withstand any attack!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《無恐》恐れずに立ち向かいます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Fearless》
I will face them without fear!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《特技》私の活躍、見ていて下さい！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Special Skill》
Please watch me in action!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《特技》秘めたる技を解放します！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Special Skill》
Unleashing my hidden skill!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《特技》限界を超えた力、今お見せします！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Special Skill》 
 I will show you a power surpassing my limits!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《癒活》この戦いに癒しの加護を！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Restoration》
I grant you the blessing of restoration in this battle!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《癒活》癒しの力で、窮地を越えましょう！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Restoration》
With the power of healing, we'll overcome this!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《重化》敵の体勢を崩してみせます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Heavy》
I'll break the enemy's stance!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《重化》早々に倒れさせてみせます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Heavy》
I'll make them fall as quickly as possible!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《集中》詠唱に集中します！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Concentration》
Concentrate on the incantation!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《集中》魔力を最大限に高めます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Concentration》
Maximizing magical power!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《集視》ご安心を、私が前に出ます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Attraction》
Rest assured, I will take the lead!</COL>

column3: <COL B6FF01>《集視》覚者さま、私が敵を引き受けます！</COL>
column4:
<COL B6FF01>《Attraction》
Arisen, I will take on the enemy!</COL>

column3: Fn Fn――　Fn　Fn――　（鼻歌）
column4: Hm-hmm, hm-hmmm...

column3: あそこに敵がいます！
column4: There, an enemy!

column3: あの敵は、まずいですね――
column4: That one looks like trouble...

column3: いいものが見つかるといいですね

We will surely protect you

column4: It's not going well...

Oh, with this,

Good night, Dear Arisen.

column4: It's an honor to meet you!

Are you preparing to sortie?

この場所は――
なにかありそうですね

This place...
There's something nearby.

I'm going to finish them off

This enemy...

We've traveled quite a bit

column3: ご友人がお見えです

column3: さあ、やりましょう！
column4: Come on, let's do this!

column3: し、しくじりました――！
column4: I...I messed up!

column3: そこに修練敵がいます！
column4: There is a training enemy there!

column3: そちらに行きます、お待ちを！
column4: I'll come to you, please wait!

column3: そんな、まさか――！

column3: ともに参りましょう

column3: なるほど――　ふむ――

column4: Yes, what is it?

column3: はい！
column4: Yes!

column3: まだ充分、戦えます
column4: I've plenty of fight left in me.

column3: やりましたね！
column4: We did it!

column3: ゆっくりして下さい、覚者さま

column3: よろしくお願いします

column3: アイテムを発見しました
column4: Item found.

column3: ダウンしました！ 一気にたたみ掛けましょう！
column4:
It's down!

column3: 一層お役に立ってみせます！
column4: I'll show you I can be useful!

column3: 了解！

column3: 今お助けします！
column4: I will aid you at once!

column3: 今です、覚者さま！

column3: 今回のエリア探索は、とても良い成果を上げることができました！

We've had very good results

Rest is also an important preparation

Before you run out of stamina,

column3: 倒しました！

先日、他の覚者さまに

I recently received a compliment

I recently accompanied another Arisen

I will gather power and attack!

Granting attributes!

覚者さま

Ser Arisen,

column4:

column3: あ、あれと戦うなんて――

W...we couldn't possibly
be fighting that thing...!

column4:

are you possibly heading out, ser?

column4: Ah... I'm losing strength...

column4: Ah, geez!

column4: O-over there!

column4:

column4:
Um...
I think someone's here.

column4: Erm, it's an honor to meet you.

column4:
Um, are you going out?

column4:
Erm, the other day,
another Arisen complimented me on this attire.

column4: Erm, there's a training enemy out there--

column4: Umm, what's the matter?

column4:
This foe...
It might be troublesome...

column4:
There are enemies

column3:
ありがとうございました――
身に付けた力で、もっと覚者さまに
頼っていただけるように、頑張ります――

Thank you very much...
I will work hard so that you, Arisen,

Argh...

column4: I hope it turns out delicious...

Huh!?

It looks furious!

column4: I hope I can be of assistance...

If you're tired,
you should get some rest.

か、覚者さま――もっとお役にたてるように
